overall_summary: The ElizaOS ecosystem is transitioning towards autonomous revenue-generating agents with the announcement of v3, while managing security risks following a series of community phishing attacks and token migration closures.
key_facts: The ai16z token migration period has officially closed as of April 20, 2026.
ElizaOS v3 is nearing release and will feature capabilities for agents to generate revenue for users.
Elisym Labs released @elisym/plugin-elizaos, enabling agents to operate as paid providers on a decentralized marketplace using Nostr protocols.
ElizaOK was clarified as being built on ElizaCloud with no current separate token.
Security vulnerabilities in the open-source application were privately disclosed to maintainers by researcher kullai on April 18.
A memory management bug in InMemoryDatabaseAdapter.updateMemories was resolved to fix roomId handling.
The core framework implemented a shared utils/batch-queue system to optimize high-concurrency tasks.
The project team redirected social media attention to Milady development as a current priority over Twitter activity.
open_questions: Can AI agents solve serious hacking problems via enhancing security keys in DEFI space?
Any tips for making minimax token plan key the provider for eliza?

twitter_news_highlights.claim: Shaw hinted at AGI developments and expressed a belief that agents should help people make money.

new_issues_prs.title: V2.0.0 release
new_issues_prs.author: odilitime
new_issues_prs.significance: Major modernization of repo automation, CI, and multi-language support for the v2.0.0 milestone.

new_issues_prs.title: feat(core): shared batch-queue drains and bounded knowledge embeddings
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Introduces a unified concurrency and retry system for task handling across the framework.

new_issues_prs.title: Delegation chains for autonomous agents
new_issues_prs.author: aeoess
new_issues_prs.significance: Proposed a granular trust model for agents including spend limits and scoped authority.

overall_focus.claim: Development focus has been on improving framework stability, resolving memory management bugs, and standardizing agent-to-agent commerce protocols.

discord_updates.summary: Focus on token migration closure, ElizaOS v3 revenue features, and clarification of the ElizaOK/ElizaCloud ecosystem relationship.
Announcement and naming convention adjustment for the Elisym plugin, allowing agents to be paid in SOL via Nostr.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users reported significant losses due to phishing scams involving fake support tickets and airdrop links.
Inquiry regarding the long inactivity of the official Twitter account was met with a redirected priority to dev work.

strategic_insights.insight: The release of ElizaOS v3 and Elisym integration points to a strategic transition from chat-based agents to autonomous economic actors.
implications_or_questions: How will the revenue-generating features impact the utility and demand for the $ELIZAOS token?

strategic_insights.insight: Persistent phishing attacks and the closure of migration windows are creating friction and financial losses for the community.
implications_or_questions: Should the project establish more formal security disclosure guidelines and anti-scam communication channels?

market_analysis.observation: ElizaOS tokens are currently confirmed as deployed on Solana, BSC, and Base chains, but staking is not available.
market_analysis.relevance: Provides clarity on asset availability while managing holder expectations regarding yields.
